lifecycle.note: Recurring friction signals surfaced across customer success and delivery capacity.
Leadership aligned around the need to clarify whether friction is structural or transitional.
Initial structure map created for handoff, ownership, and escalation patterns.
Evidence is sufficient to refine operating-model options, but not yet sufficient to activate.

signals.label: Delivery Bottleneck
signals.note: Repeated cycle-time slippage across priority accounts.

signals.label: Ownership Ambiguity
signals.note: Escalations repeatedly return to executive review.

signals.label: Latent Capacity Gap
signals.note: Unwatched signal emerging from inconsistent handoff language.

candidateBeliefs.statement: The constraint is shifting from demand generation to operating-model capacity.

evidence.summary: Three delivery teams reported the same escalation pattern across unrelated projects.
Cycle-time variance increased where ownership boundaries were least explicit.

confidence.rationale: Signals are recurrent and directionally consistent, but structural cause has not yet been fully qualified.

integrationSlots.label: SCOUT origin
integrationSlots.description: Future SCOUT ingestion will detect and cluster recurring operating-model signals.

integrationSlots.label: QDS calibration
integrationSlots.description: Future QDS execution will qualify whether the evidence supports activation or refinement.

integrationSlots.label: Docente learning capture
integrationSlots.description: Future Docente integration will preserve the learning generated by the decision path.
